hook.text: Genitive is the second most common case in Russian, after nominative. It does three big jobs: possession (whose?), absence (no X / without X), and quantity (of X — used with numbers and words like много). It's also required by a long list of prepositions. Master genitive and you've unlocked a huge chunk of the language.

why.text: ⚠ Genitive is the case that trips up learners most consistently. Not because the endings are hard, but because genitive is required in so many contexts — нет + genitive, numbers 2-4 + genitive, после/без/у/от/до + genitive, and possessive phrases. Drilling genitive widely is the single highest-return case activity at A1 level.

cards.title: Genitive singular endings
cards.body: Genitive singular has clear, consistent endings by gender. Masculine: add -А (hard) or -Я (soft). Feminine: А→Ы or А→И (after Г К Х Ж Ш Щ Ч). Neuter: О→А, Е→Я.

examples.meaning: book → of the book (А→И after Г)

cards.title: Possession — X of Y / Y's X
cards.body: Russian expresses possession with the noun in genitive after the possessed item. There's no word for 'apostrophe-s' — just the genitive ending. книга брата = the book of (the) brother = brother's book.

cards.title: Negation — нет + genitive
cards.body: After нет (there is no), the noun goes into genitive. This extends to У меня нет (I don't have) — the thing you lack is genitive. Compare: У меня есть книга (nominative — I have a book) vs У меня нет книги (genitive — I don't have a book).

cards.title: Prepositions that take genitive
cards.body: A large set of prepositions always require genitive. The most common at A1: без (without), после (after), у (at/by — possession), от (from), до (until/up to), из (from/out of), около (near).

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I don't have a book'?
questions.explanation: У меня нет + genitive. книга → книги. нет requires genitive.

questions.prompt: машина сестры — what does this mean?
questions.explanation: машина сестры = the car of (the) sister = sister's car. сестра → сестры (feminine: А→Ы).

cards.title: ⚠ нет + genitive is a top error site
cards.body: The switch from nominative (У меня есть книга) to genitive (У меня нет книги) when negating possession is one of the most commonly tested and most commonly wrong points in Russian. The pattern is: positive = nominative; negative = genitive. Drill it until it's automatic — don't reason through it each time.

examples.meaning: He doesn't have a car. (машина → машины)

cards.title: Genitive plural — frequency note
cards.body: You've already met genitive plural with numbers 5+ and words like много/мало. Masculine genitive plural: add -ОВ/-ЕВ (домов, столов). Feminine and neuter: zero ending (книг, слов). There are many exceptions — but for the words you know so far, the pattern holds.

questions.prompt: Give the genitive singular of книга:
questions.explanation: книга → книги. Feminine А→И (spelling rule after Г).

questions.prompt: Give the genitive singular of дом:
How do you say 'coffee without sugar'?
How do you say 'after the lesson'?
У меня нет ___. (время — time). Genitive form?
questions.explanation: время → времени. Neuter special declension: -Е ending in genitive.
книга → книг. Feminine genitive plural: zero ending (drop А).
брат → брата (genitive). книга брата = book of brother = brother's book.

rules: Genitive singular: masculine +А/Я, feminine А→Ы/И, neuter О→А
Possession: noun in genitive after the possessed item (книга брата = brother's book)
⚠ нет + genitive — always. У меня нет книги (I have no book)
Key genitive prepositions: без, после, у, от, до, из, около
summary.nextLesson: Next: Dative case — indirect objects, age, and verbs that require the dative.
